Scenario.title: Maroochy Water Breach: Energy Grid Sabotage via Physical and Digital Exploitation
Scenario.overview: Energy Standard A sophisticated attack on energy infrastructure mirrors the infamous Maroochy Water Breach, leveraging physical device compromise and digital persistence to disrupt grid operations. Attackers exploited weak configurations, stolen hardware, and credential harvesting to execute large-scale disruptions.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Weak passwords", "Improper configuration", "Device theft", "Autonomous system numbers (ASNs)", "Custom APIs", "SSL Certificates and Attribution"]
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: ["Loss or Theft of equipment", "Man in The Middle (MiTM)", "Application compromise", "Vulnerability exploit", "Malware", "Brute-force attack"]

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Attackers physically stole energy infrastructure devices (RTUs/PLCs) to gain direct access, exploiting supply chain vulnerabilities to replace hardware with tampered components.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-445: Malicious Logic Insertion via Configuration Management Manipulation
stage.description: Malware was injected into proprietary energy grid APIs through compromised devices, enabling remote execution of malicious payloads on industrial control systems.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A rootkit was installed via misconfigured ICS settings, creating persistent backdoor access to maintain long-term control over compromised systems.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC-226: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: Low-privilege operator credentials were brute-forced to escalate privileges, granting full administrative control over critical infrastructure systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Forged SSL certificates encrypted C2 traffic, masking command-and-control communications while evading network monitoring tools.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: ASN routing tables were manipulated to isolate critical energy systems, triggering cascading failures that mirrored the Maroochy sewage overflow incident.

Scenario.summary: The Maroochy-style attack exploited both physical and digital weaknesses in energy infrastructure, combining device theft, malware deployment, and credential abuse to achieve operational disruption. Attackers leveraged known vulnerabilities and misconfigurations to maintain persistent access and evade detection.
